title: US Fintech Upstart Prepares National Bank Charter Application
text: Upstart announced its strategic move to apply for a national bank charter. The fintech company will submit an application to the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) and the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) to establish an insured national bank. This regulatory move marks a significant milestone in Upstart's evolution as a lending platform. The charter will allow Upstart to reduce operational, regulatory, and financial costs and complexity for itself and for third-party capital sources. Upstart Bank, N.A. will be able to access deposit funding and lend to consumers directly via a single, consistent rate and fee structure. Banks, credit unions, and institutional funds will continue to be the primary capital source for loans on the platform. The proposed CEO of the new bank is Annie Delgado, Upstart's chief risk officer.
